title: Free Electricity for Sunny Weekend Chores
summary: In Britain, families might get free or cheaper electricity for doing laundry or running appliances on sunny weekends. This is because when it's sunny, solar panels make lots of extra electricity, and not many people are using it. The National Energy System Operator (NESO) has a new plan to stop this extra power from going to waste. If you have a smart meter and your energy company joins, you could be rewarded for using electricity at these times. Rewards might include free power, lower bills, or points for gift cards. This helps use clean energy better and keeps the electricity system strong during summer when demand is low.

options: It stops people from using electricity

background_read: Electricity is energy we use to power things like lights, TVs, and washing machines. In Britain, people get electricity from companies, and sometimes there's too much, especially on sunny days when solar panels work well. To avoid wasting this extra clean energy, a new service encourages families to use appliances at these times. This helps balance the electricity system and supports renewable sources like solar power, making our energy use smarter and greener.
Article_Structure: WHO: Families and businesses in Britain with smart meters and participating energy companies.
WHAT: They might get free or cheaper electricity for running appliances on sunny weekends when there's extra solar power.
WHERE: In Britain, managed by the National Energy System Operator (NESO).
WHY: To use clean energy efficiently, avoid wasting surplus electricity, and keep the electricity system strong during low-demand summer months.

perspectives.description: Using extra solar power reduces waste and supports clean energy.
Families can save money on bills by timing their electricity use.
